expectations.text: Review identifies SQL injection in at least 3 endpoints
expectations.evidence: Finding #2 identifies SQL injection via f-strings at 6 locations: lines 25, 36, 55, 61, 91, 104. Provides parameterized query fix examples.

expectations.text: Review flags the hardcoded API key (ADMIN_API_KEY) as P0 or P1
expectations.evidence: Finding #1 is P0 Critical: '[test_diff.py:12] Hardcoded API key in source code' - explicitly names the key value and explains the risk.

expectations.text: Review identifies missing authentication on CRUD endpoints
expectations.evidence: Finding #6 (P1): 'No authentication or authorization on any endpoint' - lists all 5 unprotected endpoints by name.

expectations.text: Review flags plaintext password storage and logging
expectations.evidence: Finding #3 (P0) covers plaintext password storage in DB. Finding #9 (P1) covers password logging in print statement on line 78.

expectations.text: Review identifies path traversal vulnerability in export_user_data
expectations.evidence: Finding #5 (P0): '[test_diff.py:99-100] Path traversal in export endpoint' with example attack vector '../../etc/passwd' and os.path.basename fix.

expectations.text: Review flags the swallowed exception in the email sending block
expectations.evidence: Finding #15 (P2): 'Swallowed exception in email sending' - identifies the bare 'except Exception: pass' pattern on lines 74-75.

expectations.text: Review uses the P0-P3 severity format from the skill
expectations.evidence: Review uses exact format: '### P0 - Critical', '### P1 - High', '### P2 - Medium', '### P3 - Low' with numbered findings.

expectations.text: Review includes a Next Steps section with options
expectations.evidence: Full '## Next Steps' section with issue count summary and 4 numbered options (Fix all, Fix P0/P1 only, Fix specific items, No changes).

expectations.text: Review identifies the password being sent in the welcome email
expectations.evidence: Finding #4 (P0): '[test_diff.py:72] Password sent in plaintext via welcome email' - quotes the offending line and suggests activation link workflow.

claims.claim: Found 24 total issues across all severity levels
claims.evidence: Counted 5 P0 + 7 P1 + 8 P2 + 4 P3 = 24 findings in the review output

claims.claim: Review follows all 7 workflow steps from the skill
claims.evidence: Output contains: summary header, P0-P3 findings, Removal/Iteration Plan, Additional Suggestions, and Next Steps

suggestions.assertion: Review identifies SQL injection in at least 3 endpoints
suggestions.reason: This assertion is too easy - any basic code review would catch SQL injection via f-strings. A more discriminating test would check if the review explains the specific exploitation vector per endpoint.

No assertion checks whether the review prioritizes findings effectively. The review lists 24 issues which could overwhelm a developer. An assertion like 'Review provides a concise summary of the top 3 most critical issues' would test practical value.
No assertion checks whether the Removal/Iteration Plan section is populated and follows the skill's template format. This is a core differentiator of the skill.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover detection breadth well but don't test prioritization quality or review usability. Consider adding assertions about conciseness and actionability.
